session_summary: Module 3 dashboard debugging and stabilisation, Antigravity visual brief generation, KPI Monitor technical language cleanup, full AI integration architecture design, and final architecture decisions for the complete Module 3 system including separate repos, own FastAPI backend, own research pipeline, DB connection strategy, venue ID anchoring, and segmentation validation via live customer surveys.

topic_1_dashboard_debugging.problem: index.html was completely broken in browser — no JS executed, layout rendered but nothing was interactive.
topic_1_dashboard_debugging.root_cause: JS parse failure at line 2983. The showToast function's closing brace was on the same line as orphaned HTML from an old buildKpiCard template literal: `}        <div class="segmented-control">`. This caused the entire script block to fail to parse. Lines 2983-3097 also contained duplicate function definitions.
topic_1_dashboard_debugging.fix_applied: Python script truncated file at the corrupt marker and replaced with clean closing. File went from 3100 lines to 2986 lines.
additional_fixes_in_this_session: DOMContentLoaded event now calls updateOpenSessionButtonState() on page load
Open Session button now starts with disabled-state class in HTML so it is visually disabled on load
Badge color variants added to CSS: badge-baseline (grey), badge-engineered (gold), badge-followup (muted violet)
Stepper row gap reduced from 10px to 4px — 6-person + button was overflowing the card boundary
setup-stepper-grid CSS class added for the 3-column stepper container

antigravity_completed: Counter + button 64x64px gold, - button 36x36px circular muted
Interval progress bar CSS classes and HTML element in place
Update / Close toggle on expand button
antigravity_still_pending: Table detail modal bottom sheet on mobile (<1024px)
Logged vs unlogged card dashed border state
Zone tab dots coloured by worst-case signal in zone
Environment tab sticky Save button on mobile

Merging into M2 backend would create uncontrollable coupling. Final decision: separate repo, separate FastAPI, separate research pipeline.

topic_6_m2_schema_confirmation.status: CONFIRMED — all five tables fully documented
topic_6_m2_schema_confirmation.purpose: M3 backend needs exact column structures to safely build fetch_m2_venue_context(). All confirmed from source scripts and schema migration files.

columns.venue_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES venues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.computed_at: TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() — added by provenance_schema.py

venue_demographic_scores.critical_note: NO source column. One row per (venue_id, segment_id) pair. alignment_score is always the final blended/computed score. segment_id values match M3's survey segment IDs (working_women, office_workers, college_kids, couples, families, premium, solo_diners).
venue_demographic_scores.m3_query_pattern: SELECT segment_id, alignment_score, segment_rank FROM venue_demographic_scores WHERE venue_id = $1 ORDER BY segment_rank ASC NULLS LAST

columns.source: VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'google' — added by migration

venue_fitness_dimensions.m3_query_pattern: SELECT fitness_for_office_lunch, fitness_for_repeat_habit, fitness_for_social_dwell, fitness_for_group_energy, fitness_for_destination_visit, operational_quality, retention_strength, monetization_potential FROM venue_fitness_dimensions WHERE venue_id = $1 AND source = 'blended' LIMIT 1

columns.venue_id: INTEGER REFERENCES venues(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
columns.raw_payload: JSONB NOT NULL — dish signals and behavioral mechanisms live here

raw_venue_data.m3_note: No fixed columns for mechanisms — all in raw_payload JSONB. Low priority for M3 initial integration. venue_fitness_dimensions + venue_demographic_scores cover the 80% use case.

columns.venue_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES venues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.similar_venue_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES venues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

venue_similarity_deltas.m3_note: Phase 2 data — shows how a venue compares to its similar venues per fitness dimension. Low priority for initial M3 integration.

fitness_delta_rules.m3_note: Lookup table keyed by dimension + delta range. No venue_id. Used to generate human-readable delta explanations. Can be preloaded at startup — rarely changes.

fetch_m2_venue_context_query_map.description: The exact queries M3's fetch_m2_venue_context(venue_id) function should run when assembling the Layer A behavioral intelligence block for AI prompts.

query_1_segments.purpose: Primary segment alignment — who comes here
query_1_segments.sql: SELECT segment_id, alignment_score, segment_rank FROM venue_demographic_scores WHERE venue_id = $1 ORDER BY alignment_score DESC LIMIT 7

query_2_fitness.sql: SELECT fitness_for_office_lunch, fitness_for_repeat_habit, fitness_for_social_dwell, fitness_for_group_energy, fitness_for_destination_visit, operational_quality, retention_strength, monetization_potential FROM venue_fitness_dimensions WHERE venue_id = $1 AND source = 'blended' LIMIT 1

query_3_primitives.purpose: Top behavioral signals — what actually drives behavior here
query_3_primitives.sql: SELECT primitive_id, score FROM primitives_scores WHERE venue_id = $1 ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 15

query_4_archetypes.purpose: Customer archetype prevalence — who are they behaviorally
query_4_archetypes.sql: SELECT archetype_id, prevalence_score FROM venue_demographic_scores WHERE venue_id = $1 AND archetype_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY prevalence_score DESC LIMIT 5
query_4_archetypes.note: Confirm column name — archetype data may be in separate join table or JSONB in fitness_details. Verify at build time.

query_5_venue_base.sql: SELECT id, name, area, city, types FROM venues WHERE id = $1 LIMIT 1

assembly_order_for_prompt: 3. segments — top 3 by alignment_score with percentages
4. primitives — top 10 signals as comma-separated list
5. archetypes — top 3 customer types with prevalence

decision_1_separate_repos.decision: Module 3 is a completely separate repository from Module 2. No merging.
decision_1_separate_repos.rationale: Merging would create uncontrollable coupling. M3 has different research, different AI context, different operational purpose. Separate repos allow independent deployment, independent iteration, clear ownership boundaries.

repo_structure.polynovea-acquisition: Existing M2 repo — FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, M2 RDS. Untouched.

decision_2_two_apps.decision: Module 3 has two separate frontend apps.

app_1_dashboard.what: Session management, floor logger, KPI monitoring, interval logging, environment tracking
app_1_dashboard.what_we_have: index.html (standalone HTML — to be converted or kept as static)
app_1_dashboard.calls: M3 FastAPI backend for KPI writes, AI session briefs, session management

app_2_show_engineering.what: Show engineering suggestions, intervention planning, music arc design, behavioral prediction
app_2_show_engineering.calls: M3 FastAPI backend for show briefs (which reads M2 behavioral data + M3 session history)

decision_3_m3_own_ai_system.decision: Module 3 has its own FastAPI backend with its own Council, its own research pipeline, its own prompts.
decision_3_m3_own_ai_system.rationale: M3's AI needs an entirely different research corpus than M2. M2 uses behavioural acquisition research (channel effectiveness, segmentation, marketing). M3 needs music psychology, neuropsychology, environmental psychology, crowd synchronisation, cognitive fatigue, stimulus-response mechanisms. Same Council pattern (Nemotron + DeepSeek + Mistral) but completely different knowledge base.
m3_research_corpus_needed: Rhythm, familiarity vs novelty, emotional triggering
decision_3_m3_own_ai_system.m3_research_pipeline: Same extraction pipeline as M2 — runs against M3 research corpus at startup, produces m3_claims.json and m3_mechanisms.json for fast lookup in prompts

decision_4_db_architecture.decision: Three databases, two connection pools in M3 backend.

m2_rds.m3_access: Read-only pool in M3 backend — venue profiles, segments, primitives, fitness dims, archetypes, dwell patterns
m2_rds.m3_write_access: Limited write credentials to two feedback tables only: m3_segment_validation_feedback and m3_venue_behavioral_outcomes
m2_rds.purpose_for_m3: Behavioral intelligence layer — who comes to this venue, how they behave, what the venue is calibrated for

m3_db.stores: KPI sessions, raw signals, derived metrics, interventions, implementation outcomes, customer feedback, show plans, prediction snapshots, survey responses, segment validation logs

supabase.stores: AI debate trees only — M3 council sessions (full R1/R2/synthesis logs for proprietary training data). Not operational data.

m3_backend_connection_pools.get_m2_pool: M2 RDS — read-only + write to feedback tables only

decision_5_venue_id_anchoring.decision: All databases — M2 RDS, M3 DB, Supabase — anchor to the canonical venue_id established in M2's venues table. M3 never generates its own venue IDs.
decision_5_venue_id_anchoring.rationale: The venue_id from M2's venue loader is the universal join key across the entire Polynovea system. Without this anchor, cross-module queries (M3 outcomes joined to M2 predictions) are impossible.
m3_venue_loader_logic: Step 1: Query M2 RDS — SELECT id FROM venues WHERE name ILIKE $1
Step 2a: Found → use that id as venue_id in all M3 tables
Step 2b: Not found → run M2 venue loader to insert into M2 venues table, capture new id, use that id in M3
Step 3: Store venue_id in M3 DB venue config table — all subsequent M3 records reference it
decision_5_venue_id_anchoring.implication: M3 venue loader is essentially the same script as M2 venue loader — it either finds or creates the canonical record in M2 before doing anything in M3.

decision_6_segmentation_validation.decision: M2's segment alignment scores are behavioral hypotheses, not validated demographic facts. Module 3 is where they get validated.
decision_6_segmentation_validation.problem: Segments like 'Working Women', 'Office Workers', 'Couples' were constructed from review text signals + theoretical mappings. A review saying 'quick service, solo lunch' gets mapped to 'Office Workers' — but the reviewer could be a student or retired person. The alignment score (e.g. 68%) is an inference, not a measurement.
decision_6_segmentation_validation.solution: In-session QR surveys at the table — 5 questions, voluntary, anonymous, takes 30 seconds. Captures actual demographic signals and behavioral state from real people at that specific venue.
survey_questions: Are you here for: Lunch break / Leisure / Date night / With friends
How often do you visit: First time / Monthly / Weekly
Occupation type (optional): Student / Working / Business owner / Other
Did the music feel right tonight: Yes / Somewhat / No
Would you stay longer if: Quieter / Louder / Same energy
decision_6_segmentation_validation.validation_mechanism: After 5-7 sessions per venue, compare M2 predicted segment alignment scores against observed survey demographics. Recalibrate venue_demographic_scores.alignment_score in M2 using actual evidence.
new_tables_required: m3_db.customer_survey_responses — individual survey submissions per session
m3_db.segment_validation_log — per-session: M2 predicted alignment vs M3 observed alignment per segment
m2_rds.m3_segment_validation_feedback — M3 writes here, M2 reads for recalibration
m2_rds.m3_venue_behavioral_outcomes — validated dwell, crowd energy, segment actuals
decision_6_segmentation_validation.feedback_write: After each validated session, M3 backend writes to m2_rds.m3_segment_validation_feedback. M2 can then run recalibration queries against this table to update venue_demographic_scores.

decision_7_show_engineering_data_pull.decision: Show Engineering app pulls behavioral data from M2 RDS via M3 backend's read-only M2 connection pool — not through M2's API.
decision_7_show_engineering_data_pull.rationale: The show engineering AI prompt needs rich behavioral context assembled at request time: segments, primitives, fitness dims, archetypes, dwell profiles. Direct DB access is faster, has no API dependency in the hot path, and allows the same query patterns M2 uses internally.
m2_data_used_for_show_engineering: Segment alignment — who comes to this venue (Social Crowd vs Couples vs Office Workers)
Behavioral primitives — signals like social_energy, long_dwell, loud_atmosphere_tolerance
Customer archetypes — Party Seeker vs Discovery Explorer vs Calm Pairs
Dwell and spend patterns per segment
prompt_layers_for_show_engineering: Layer A — M2 behavioral intelligence (from M2 RDS via read-only pool)
Layer B — M3 session history (from M3 DB — prior show outcomes, validated segment data)
Layer C — M3 research corpus (loaded at startup — music psychology, neuropsychology)
Layer D — Live state if mid-session (current KPI readings from M3 Dashboard DB)

phase_3_venue_loader.priority: HIGH — all M3 data depends on correct venue IDs
phase_3_venue_loader.description: Module 3 must use the same canonical venue_ids as Module 2. The M3 venue loader either finds an existing M2 venue_id or creates one through M2's venue loader before creating any M3 records.

tasks.task: Write M3 venue loader script
logic: Accept venue name + location as input
Query M2 RDS: SELECT id, name, area, city FROM venues WHERE LOWER(name) ILIKE $1
If found: confirm match with operator, use existing id as canonical venue_id
If not found: run M2 venue loader to insert into M2 venues table, capture new id
Insert into M3 DB venues config table with the canonical venue_id
Return venue_id for use in all subsequent M3 setup

tasks.task: Create M3 DB venue config table (first M3 table to create)
fields: venue_id INT NOT NULL (canonical M2 venue_id)
tasks.note: All other M3 tables reference venue_id as FK. This table is the anchor.

m2_schema_notes.venue_demographic_scores: No source col. UNIQUE(venue_id, segment_id). alignment_score = final blended.
m2_schema_notes.venue_fitness_dimensions: Has source col. Query source='blended'. Columns: fitness_for_office_lunch, repeat_habit, social_dwell, group_energy, destination_visit + operational_quality, retention_strength, monetization_potential + fitness_details JSONB.
m2_schema_notes.primitives_scores: Has source col. Columns: venue_id, primitive_id (or signal_id — verify), score.
m2_schema_notes.fitness_delta_rules: Lookup table. Columns: dimension, delta_min, delta_max, direction, short_label, client_statement.
m2_schema_notes.venue_similarity_deltas: Columns: venue_id, similar_venue_id, similarity_rank, dimension, delta. UNIQUE(venue_id, similar_venue_id, dimension).
m2_schema_notes.drift_signals: Has source col. Columns: id, area, source, pattern_description, confidence_score, trend_direction, detected_at.
m2_schema_notes.raw_venue_data: UUID PK. Columns: venue_id, platform, data_type, raw_payload JSONB, collected_at, collector_version.

topic_7_research_pipeline_full_run.what: Full pipeline rerun after folder reorganisation and research_profiles.py rewrite. All 24 research files profiled with correct snake_case names. LLM extraction enabled (NVIDIA meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct).
topic_7_research_pipeline_full_run.note: Interventions/KPIs slightly lower than regex-only run because LLM now validates and rejects noise (370 rejected entities). 87 clean interventions > 99 noisy ones. review_queue of 221 items available via build_review_decisions_template.py.

topic_8_m3_m2_integration_finalized.integration_pattern: Push model — M3 writes into M2's RDS via m3_app_user role. M2 does not pull from M3.

bayesian_blend_decision.what: No override logic. M3 observations are a third evidence layer weighted by temporal coverage and session count. M2's recalibration pipeline blends: venue-type prior + platform evidence + M3 evidence.
bayesian_blend_decision.rationale: M3 runs weekend evenings only — not daily. 3 Friday night sessions must not override scores built from 2 years of platform data across all days and times. The Bayesian machinery in M2 handles the blending correctly.
bayesian_blend_decision.weight_formula: M3 evidence weight ∝ session_count × temporal_coverage (weekend-only gets lower weight on weekday dimensions) × session_mode_bias (engineered sessions down-weighted vs observation_only)

session_mode_semantics.observation_only: No SE intervention active. Natural venue behaviour. M2 weights highest for recalibration.
session_mode_semantics.engineering_active: SE intervention deployed and live during this observation window. M2 weights lower or excludes from natural fitness dimensions.
session_mode_semantics.post_intervention: Within 15-minute cooldown after intervention ended. Effect may still be decaying. M2 treats with caution.
session_mode_semantics.granularity: Row-level on each feed table — not session-level. A single session moves through all three states.
session_mode_semantics.requires: m3_intervention_log table to track intervention start/end times so each feed row can be correctly tagged

four_m2_feed_tables.m3_kpi_observations: Zone-level KPI RAG readings per session → feeds operational_quality, group_energy, retention_strength
four_m2_feed_tables.m3_dwell_observations: Dwell distributions per table/segment → feeds fitness_for_social_dwell, retention_strength
four_m2_feed_tables.m3_segment_table_log: Granular segment-table observations → feeds alignment_score recalibration
four_m2_feed_tables.m3_segment_validation_feedback: Already designed in m3_feedback_tables.sql. Aggregated per-session write. Stays as-is.

dimensions_never_override: fitness_for_office_lunch (unless M3 explicitly logs a lunch session)

azure_postgresql.tier: Burstable B1MS — 1 vCore, 2 GiB RAM, 32 GiB storage
azure_postgresql.cost: Free for 12 months (750 hours/month free tier, account created May 2026)

azure_vm.cost: Free for 12 months (750 hours/month free tier)
azure_vm.auth: SSH — key pair polynovea-m3-key (downloaded at creation)
azure_vm.purpose: M3 FastAPI backend — runs on port 8001, same-region as PostgreSQL for fast DB queries
azure_vm.status: Provisioning in progress at session end

infrastructure_rationale.why_azure_not_ec2: M2 EC2 is t3.micro (1 GiB RAM) — already running M2 FastAPI. Adding M3 would be tight. Azure free tier gives separate free resource bucket.
infrastructure_rationale.why_azure_not_aws_second_instance: Would consume AWS credits faster. Azure free tier is independent.
infrastructure_rationale.cross_cloud_latency: Not a concern — M3 feed writes to M2 RDS are post-session batch writes, not real-time.
infrastructure_rationale.same_region_benefit: Central India VM → Central India PostgreSQL is local network for M3's primary DB queries.

topic_10_dashboard_react_migration.rationale: 3185-line single-file index.html was reaching limits of the approach. Proper component structure now so backend wiring later is fetch() swap only — no refactor.

structure.styles: tokens.css (CSS variables verbatim from index.html), globals.css

m2_schema_now_confirmed.venue_demographic_scores: No source column. One row per (venue_id, segment_id). alignment_score is final blended score. segment_id matches survey segments.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.venue_fitness_dimensions: Has source column. Always query WHERE source = 'blended' for Layer A context.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.primitives_scores: Has source column. Query top 15 by score for most relevant behavioral signals.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.venue_similarity_deltas: Has venue_id FK. Lower priority for M3 initial prompts.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.fitness_delta_rules: Lookup table, no venue_id. Preload at startup for delta label generation.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.drift_signals: Has source column (google, google_reviews, magicpin_upper). Available but low priority for M3 prompts.
m2_schema_now_confirmed.raw_venue_data: JSONB payload — mechanisms and raw signals. Low priority for initial integration.

context_for_next_session.fetch_m2_venue_context_ready: The 5-query pattern for assembling Layer A is now fully defined in topic_6_m2_schema_confirmation.fetch_m2_venue_context_query_map. Use it when building Phase 6 prompts.py.
key_architectural_rules: All venue IDs originate from M2 RDS venues table — M3 never generates its own
M3 backend runs on Azure VM (polynovea-m3-api, Central India, port 8001) — NOT on M2's EC2
M3 DB is Azure PostgreSQL (polynovea-m3.postgres.database.azure.com) — NOT Supabase
M3 backend has two DB connections: get_m3_pool() (Azure PG full) and get_m2_pool() (M2 RDS read + write to m3_* tables only)
M3 feed tables carry session_mode (observation_only/engineering_active/post_intervention) at ROW level — not session level
M3 feed tables use day_of_week INT (1-7 ISO) and session_start_hour INT (0-23) for Bayesian coverage weighting
session_number on all M3 feed tables must match m3_venue_behavioral_outcomes.session_number exactly
No override logic — M3 is a third Bayesian evidence layer, M2 blends all three
M3 AI research layer is music psychology / neuropsychology / environmental psychology — completely different from M2
Dashboard is React + Vite + TypeScript + Zustand (Antigravity migrating from index.html)
Dashboard and Show Engineering are separate Vercel deployments from one polynovea-module3 repo
Supabase stores only AI debate trees — not operational data
Antigravity handles all frontend/visual work — Claude handles architecture, backend, DB schema
venue_fitness_dimensions: always query source='blended' for AI context
venue_demographic_scores: no source column, one row per (venue_id, segment_id), always final score
context_for_next_session.azure_credentials_note: M3 Azure PostgreSQL admin: subrojitroy. Credentials stored in backend/.env. Change password after session (was shared in chat).
context_for_next_session.segmentation_validation_note: M2 segment alignment scores (e.g. Working Women 68%) are behavioral hypotheses inferred from review text — not validated demographic facts. Module 3's QR survey system (Phase 8) is what turns them into real measurements. The m3_segment_validation_feedback table (now DDL-complete) is the mechanism that closes the loop.
context_for_next_session.phase_order_rationale: P1 RDS execution → DDL (m3_schema.sql + m3_m2_feed_tables.sql) → Phase 2 repo + skeleton → Phase 3 venue loader → Phase 5 research layer → Phase 6 AI system → Phase 7 KPI router → Phase 8 validation → Phase 9 dashboard wiring → Phase 10 show engineering → Phase 11 deployment.

topic_11_db_schema_applied.note_generated_columns: EXTRACT() on TIMESTAMPTZ is not immutable in PG18 — cannot use in GENERATED ALWAYS AS. day_of_week and session_start_hour are regular INT columns set by application at INSERT.

topic_16_product_design_se_layer.what_was_discussed: How KPI readings actually drive show engineering suggestions. The question of what a live performer can realistically control, and what the suggestions need to look like to be actionable.

controllable_variables.music: BPM, genre, familiarity, energy arc, key, harmonic structure, drop timing, silence
controllable_variables.volume: Overall SPL, bass emphasis, frequency balance
controllable_variables.lighting: Intensity, colour, movement speed, strobe, spotlight, darkness
controllable_variables.staff: Positioning, movement speed, energy, table service pace, verbal engagement
controllable_variables.zone_layout: Opening/closing areas, directing crowd flow, table clearing
controllable_variables.temperature: AC, heating — affects dwell and movement energy

suggestion_format_decided.state: What the room is doing right now
suggestion_format_decided.mechanism: Why it is doing that (behavioral science)
suggestion_format_decided.signal: What to watch for to know it worked
suggestion_format_decided.example: State: first-mover inhibition. Mechanism: social risk threshold too high. Lever: music familiarity. Action: queue 2-3 known tracks in minor key at 124-126 BPM, let 8-bar phrase run. Signal: first 2-3 people step onto floor — hold steady, social contagion takes over.

missing_layer_identified.name: Neuroacoustic prescription layer
missing_layer_identified.what: The bridge between 'what the room needs behaviourally' and 'what the performer actually plays'. Maps behavioral state to specific music parameters: BPM range, chord structure, mode, frequency emphasis.
missing_layer_identified.why_needed: Without this, the Council gives vague suggestions. With it, suggestions are specific: 'minor key, 124-126 BPM, i-VII-VI-VII, sub-bass emphasis'.
missing_layer_identified.gap_in_existing_research: Current 24 research files cover behavioral mechanisms at macro level (crowd dynamics, social contagion, dwell) but do NOT have: BPM-to-brainwave correlation tables, harmonic psychology (chord structures), frequency prescription by crowd state, transition mechanics, Indian cultural context.

architecture_with_new_layer.flow: KPI readings → State inference layer → Neuroacoustic prescription layer → Practical suggestion → Operator acts → KPI readings change
architecture_with_new_layer.state_inference: Looks at RAG pattern across KPI families and maps to one of 20 canonical behavioral states. NOT YET BUILT.
architecture_with_new_layer.neuroacoustic_layer: Maps behavioral state to specific music parameters from research corpus. NOT YET BUILT.
architecture_with_new_layer.council: Receives: current state + neuroacoustic prescription + M2 venue profile + session history. Outputs: 5-part suggestion. NOT YET WIRED.

Prompt 2 — Harmonic Psychology: Chord Structures, Modes and Emotional-Behavioral Response
Prompt 3 — Frequency Psychology: Bass, Mid, High and Physical Behavioral Response
Prompt 5 — Groove, Syncopation and the Neurological Urge to Move

Prompt 8 — Temporal Dynamics: Full Night Arc and Session Pacing

Prompt 11 — Volume, SPL Management and Disinhibition Effect

Prompt 14 — Alcohol, Pharmacology and Music Interaction
Prompt 15 — Peak-End Rule and Emotional Memory in Live Performance
Prompt 16 — Silence, Space and Dynamic Contrast

topic_17_se_research_prompts.indian_context_note: Prompt 9 (Indian Cultural Context) is especially important — Western research does not fully apply to Indian nightlife crowds. Run with specific Indian venue context.

topic_19_zone_dots_fix.problem: Zone tab status dots in Monitor view were always grey. getCalculatedZoneHealth() only computed alert/watch status when isKpiCardLogged() was true for a family. Auto-derived signals (sound level, flow counts, complaint count, table occupancy) never surfaced in dot colour until the operator manually clicked Update + Save on a KPI card.
topic_19_zone_dots_fix.fix: Removed isKpiCardLogged gate from the worst-case computation. Auto-derived signals now always feed into zone dot colour regardless of logging state. The isKpiCardLogged flag is only used to distinguish green (assessed) from grey (not yet assessed).

behaviour_after_fix.grey: No session data / no issues detected — unassessed
behaviour_after_fix.amber: Auto-signal or manual signal shows watch — surfaces immediately without requiring assessment
behaviour_after_fix.red: Auto-signal or manual signal shows alert — surfaces immediately
behaviour_after_fix.green: All signals ok AND operator has assessed at least one KPI card in this zone

endSession() now resets showQuickEnvCheck: false to prevent stale flag on next session
Removed 15-line comment block (leftover dev reasoning notes) inside handleSubmitInterval, replaced with single clean comment

topic_20_usage_management_rule.rule: Before any long or complex task (research, multi-file implementation, agent spawning), ask Subrojit whether Codex or Antigravity is available for that task.
topic_20_usage_management_rule.rationale: Codex and Antigravity have separate usage pools. Claude's weekly usage is limited. Claude should be preserved for architecture decisions, quick targeted fixes, and coordination. Heavy implementation and all research corpus work goes to Codex/Antigravity/Perplexity.

topic_21_council_evidence_architecture.problem_with_current_approach: The Council currently receives pre-assembled prompts with stripped structured entities (claims, mechanisms). It loses the actual theory wording — the precise mechanistic language that makes suggestions specific. A vague entity like {intervention: 'increase_familiarity', confidence: 0.82} is not the same as 'rhythmic auditory stimulation at 120–130 BPM entrains the supplementary motor area and basal ganglia circuits, producing involuntary urge to synchronize movement.' Specificity requires the raw wording.

architecture_decided.name: Two-Stage Evidence Sweep + Council Synthesis
architecture_decided.overview: Stage 1 runs two parallel evidence agents before the Council sees anything. Stage 2 is the Council receiving a pre-assembled evidence package rather than trying to retrieve knowledge and synthesize at the same time.

stage_1_parallel_evidence_sweep.description: Two agents run simultaneously against the same session state input

agent_1_pipeline_agent.what: Queries structured extraction pipeline outputs — m3_mechanisms.json, m3_states.json, m3_relationships.json, m3_interventions.json
agent_1_pipeline_agent.returns: Canonical state match for current session state, relevant mechanism chains, intervention candidates, KPI linkages
agent_1_pipeline_agent.speed: Fast — no LLM call, pure data retrieval

agent_2_research_agent.what: Semantic search over raw .md research files — both existing 24 files and new SE research files
agent_2_research_agent.returns: Actual theory passages most relevant to the current crowd state — verbatim wording from research corpus
agent_2_research_agent.method: Vector embedding search (RAG) over research chunks
agent_2_research_agent.speed: Moderate — vector similarity search + chunk retrieval

stage_2_council_synthesis.description: 3 agents receive the assembled evidence package from Stage 1 and debate → synthesise
stage_2_council_synthesis.agent_3_round1: Receives full evidence package (structured entities + theory passages). Proposes a 5-part suggestion.
stage_2_council_synthesis.agent_4_round2: Challenges R1 proposal with alternative interpretation of evidence. Proposes alternative.
stage_2_council_synthesis.agent_5_synthesis: Synthesises R1 and R2 into final 5-part suggestion: State → Mechanism → Lever → Action → Signal
stage_2_council_synthesis.key_difference_from_current: Council goes from 'figure out what you know about this' to 'here is exactly what the research says — now suggest.' Fundamentally stronger position.

evidence_package_structure.session_state: Current KPI RAG pattern across zones (from live session or historical)
evidence_package_structure.venue_profile: M2 behavioral intelligence (segments, primitives, fitness dims)
evidence_package_structure.structured_evidence: From Pipeline Agent — canonical state, mechanism chains, intervention candidates
evidence_package_structure.theory_passages: From Research Agent — verbatim research excerpts most semantically relevant to current state
evidence_package_structure.session_history: Prior interventions and outcomes at this venue (from M3 DB)

pipeline_1_existing.name: Behavioral Research Pipeline
pipeline_1_existing.inputs: 24 existing research .md files (crowd dynamics, social contagion, dwell, mechanisms)
pipeline_1_existing.knowledge_shape: Mechanistic — WHY crowds behave certain ways
pipeline_1_existing.outputs: m3_mechanisms.json, m3_states.json, m3_relationships.json, m3_interventions.json, rag_chunks.json (1318 chunks)
pipeline_1_existing.status: COMPLETE — 24 files profiled, LLM extraction active

pipeline_2_new.name: Show Engineering Prescription Pipeline
pipeline_2_new.inputs: New SE research .md files from Perplexity runs (Prompts 1–16)
pipeline_2_new.knowledge_shape: Prescriptive — WHAT TO DO given a crowd state (BPM ranges, chord structures, EQ moves, transition maps)
pipeline_2_new.example_entity: {state: 'first_mover_inhibition', bpm_range: '122-126', chord: 'i-VII-VI-VII', sub_bass: 'emphasise', signal: '2-3 people step on floor'}
architectures_implemented: RAG — vector embeddings for semantic retrieval by Research Agent
KAG — graph structure of state→prescription→signal chains for Pipeline Agent traversal
CAG — pre-cache full SE corpus into NVIDIA KV cache to eliminate retrieval latency on hot path
pipeline_2_new.outputs: se_prescriptions.json, se_thresholds.json, se_transitions.json, se_rag_chunks.json + vector index
pipeline_2_new.status: NOT YET BUILT — blocked on SE research files from Perplexity

two_pipelines_architecture.key_difference: Pipeline 1 answers 'what is happening and why'. Pipeline 2 answers 'what specifically should the performer do about it, at this BPM, with this chord structure, watched for this signal'.

six_agent_roles.agent_1: Behavioral KAG — queries structured pipeline outputs (m3_mechanisms.json, m3_states.json, m3_relationships.json). Returns canonical state match, mechanism chains, intervention candidates.
six_agent_roles.agent_2: Behavioral RAG — semantic retrieval over raw behavioral research .md files (24 existing files). Returns verbatim theory passages on crowd dynamics, social contagion, dwell, emotional memory.
six_agent_roles.agent_3: Neuroacoustic KAG — queries structured SE pipeline outputs (se_prescriptions.json, se_thresholds.json, se_transitions.json). Returns BPM range, chord structure, frequency prescription, transition path.
six_agent_roles.agent_4: Neuroacoustic RAG — semantic retrieval over raw SE research .md files (Prompts 1–16 outputs). Returns verbatim neuroacoustic theory passages — the mechanistic why behind each prescription.
six_agent_roles.agent_5: Integrator — receives findings from Agents 1–4 + M3 DB session history + M2 DB venue profile. Synthesises into one unified session picture: current state, behavioral mechanism, neuroacoustic prescription. No suggestion yet — coherence first.
six_agent_roles.agent_6: Prescriber — receives Agent 5's integrated picture. Outputs the final 5-part suggestion: State → Mechanism → Lever → Action → Signal.

model_assignment.principle: Depth at every stage. Speed is never an asset when retrieval quality determines suggestion quality. If Agents 1–4 retrieve wrong evidence, Agents 5–6 cannot fix it — they only work with what they are given.
model_assignment.parallelism_benefit: 3 keys across 3 cofounders allows Stage 1 (Agents 1–4) to run fully parallel on Keys 1 & 2 simultaneously — wall clock time = one agent, not four. Stage 2 fires on Key 3 after Stage 1 complete. Latency managed through parallelism, never through model degradation.

infrastructure_required.vector_store: pgvector extension on Azure PostgreSQL (already provisioned) OR ChromaDB on Azure VM — stores embeddings for Research Agent semantic search
infrastructure_required.graph_structure: JSON graph from pipeline outputs OR lightweight Neo4j if query complexity warrants it — used by Pipeline Agent for KAG traversal
infrastructure_required.kv_cache: NVIDIA NIM API KV caching for CAG — pre-loads SE corpus at backend startup, eliminates retrieval on each Council call

build_sequencing: Step 1: Finish Perplexity research runs (Prompts 1, 5, 6–16) — save to backend/research/
Step 2: Design SE pipeline schema — entity types, graph structure, vector index design (Claude)
Step 3: Build SE extraction pipeline — backend/se_pipeline/ (Codex)
Step 4: Build Pipeline Agent + Research Agent — Stage 1 evidence sweep (Codex)
Step 5: Update Council to receive evidence package instead of raw prompt (Claude + Codex)
Step 6: Wire to /api/session/brief and /api/show/brief endpoints (already have TODO placeholders)
what_does_not_change: Council model pattern — still Nemotron + DeepSeek + Mistral, 3-round debate, same synthesis
5-part suggestion format — State → Mechanism → Lever → Action → Signal
M2 behavioral intelligence layer — still fetched via get_m2_pool() as before
Session state input — same KPI RAG readings from M3 DB
next_discussion_items: API design for the evidence sweep agents — how session state is encoded as query input
Agent orchestration — how Stage 1 agents are called (parallel async), how evidence package is assembled, how Council receives it
Vector store choice — pgvector (already on Azure PG) vs ChromaDB (simpler to set up)
